Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace global provider registries with React context

Removes setAuthProvider/getAuthProvider and setDataProvider/getDataProvider
singletons in favor of idiomatic React patterns: authProvider passed as a
prop through App, dataProvider served via DataProviderContext/useDataProvider.
Fixes leaky auth abstraction by moving hash-token restore logic into
SupabaseAuthProvider.restoreSessionFromUrl(). Deletes the fetchers object
from queries.ts (45 lines of 1:1 wrappers) — all 17 consumer files now
call the data provider directly.

commit.message:
Fix Vercel build failure: remove invalid images property from vercel.json

Vercel's Image Optimization API (images config) is for Next.js only.
For Vite + React apps, unplugin-imagemin handles optimization at build time.
Keep only the rewrites configuration for React Router SPA.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(audio): persist duck volume across background audio track advances

AudioLayer's inner Player remounts on track advance, creating a fresh
audio element at volume 1.0. The duck state was only tracked on the DOM
element, so it was lost. Now isDuckedRef tracks playlist-level duck state,
and a MutationObserver re-applies the ducked volume when the audio element
remounts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve SSO cookie parsing issues for cross-subdomain authentication

- Fix cookie get() handler to properly parse values containing '=' characters
- Add decodeURIComponent() to handle URL-encoded cookie values
- Remove debug console.log statements from cookie handlers and auth flow

This resolves two critical SSO issues:
1. TypeError when Supabase tries to parse base64 session cookies
2. Cookies found on initial load but not on refresh

feat: Tool Loadouts / Toolbelts for Agent Tooling (#116)

* Initial plan

* Initial plan for Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature with tests

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add comprehensive Tool Loadout documentation

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add tool discovery system with auto-loadout scoping

Adds a dynamic tool discovery mechanism that allows agents to start with
a minimal toolset and discover/activate additional tools on demand. This
prevents context window overflow on small-context models (e.g. Foundry
Local 28K) while keeping all tools accessible.

New features:
- ToolDiscoveryTools: 4 kernel functions (discover_tools, activate_tool,
  list_tool_categories, list_toolbelts) for runtime tool management
- Auto-loadout selection: maps context window size to appropriate loadout
  (>=128K -> full, >=64K -> developer, <64K -> minimal)
- Loadout-aware scoping replaces crude "disable all tools" approach
- toolDiscovery plugin always preserved across all loadouts

Also fixes flaky SlidingWindowRateLimiter test with retry loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve formatting issues in SubagentRunner and ToolLoadoutTests

Remove alignment padding in switch expressions and fix import ordering
to satisfy dotnet format CI checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(growth): motor de correlações em camadas + limpeza e organização do banco

## Motor de Correlações (Camada de Atribuição)
- Adiciona 3 materialized views: mv_customer_campaign_attribution,
  mv_campaign_ltv_performance (com JOIN correto por channel), mv_cohort_retention
- Adiciona tabela campaign_performance_snapshots para histórico mensal
- Adiciona função refresh_correlation_views() e backfill_acquisition_channel_by_period()
- Adiciona external_id + external_platform em campaigns para bridge com IDs de Meta/Google
- Renames migrations duplicadas (timestamps colisão entre founders) para versões únicas

## 3 Novos Detectores de Growth (Detectores 6, 7, 8)
- divergencia_roi_canal: spend crescendo + ROI caindo vs mês anterior
- queda_retencao_cohort: retenção 30d < 50% da média histórica
- canal_alto_ltv_underinvested: ROI > 3x + LTV > 2x média + spend < R$1k

## Fixes Críticos
- capital.service.ts: corrige user_id → profile_id (score sempre zerado)
- vercel.json: corrige path do cron (/api/integrations/cron/sync → /api/cron/sync)
- cron pipeline: adiciona refresh das views + growth correlations na Fase 3 do cron

## Infra
- correlation-refresh.job.ts: job diário às 03h (refresh views) + snapshot mensal dia 1
- index.ts: registra startCorrelationRefreshJob()
- integration.controller.ts: adiciona Fase 3 no cronSync (refresh + growth correlations)
- Growth.tsx: 3 novos tipos de recomendação com cores (ROI=laranja, Retenção=vermelho, Oportunidade=verde)

feat: add --skip-existing flag to generate.js and run-from-md.js

Skips spec generation for test cases whose .spec.ts already exists in
tests/zephyr/. Tracking is implicit — testId maps directly to filename.

Usage:
  node generate.js bridge/test-cases/my-app.md --skip-existing
  node bridge/run-from-md.js bridge/test-cases/my-app.md --skip-existing

First run generates everything. Subsequent runs only pick up new test
cases added to the .md file. Broken locators are handled by auto-heal,
not regeneration.

commit.message:
Harden platform security: 20 fixes across 10 files

CRITICAL:
- Subscription check fails closed (503) instead of allowing free provisioning
- Set allowInsecureAuth: false on all new gateway instances
- Upgrade gateway token from UUID to 256-bit randomBytes
- Add subscription verification before granting gateway access
- Remove duplicate Sentry.captureException call

HIGH:
- Sanitize error messages in instance, provider-keys, chat routes
- Strip fly_app_name, fly_machine_id, fly_volume_id from API responses
- Fix OAuth env key mismatch (ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN → ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN)
- Add SELECTED_MODEL to ALLOWED_ENV_KEYS (model changes reach gateway)

MEDIUM:
- Verify checkout.session.mode === 'subscription' in webhook
- Skip gateway teardown on partial refunds (amount_refunded < amount)
- Sanitize webhook fire-and-forget provisioning error
- Normalize email in checkout rate limit key
- Add max length check on provider key submission

HARDENING:
- Sentry client: sendDefaultPii false, maskAllInputs, API key scrubbing
- CSP: remove unsafe-eval, add base-uri, worker-src, Permissions-Policy
- Delete .env.vercel.tmp with leaked credentials

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: address 6 design issues in skill

1. tasks-log.md auto-trim at 50 lines (prevent infinite growth)
2. Empty goal handling: prompt user, don't fabricate goals
3. Remove fixed output directory table (AI decides placement)
4. Remove fixed "3-5 tasks" count (use reasonable granularity)
5. Remove P0/P1/P2 priority labels (simplify to: AUTONOMOUS first, then backlog)
6. Add error handling: log failure, skip, continue

commit.message:
Fix all footer and logo links to use absolute URLs

next/link with relative paths breaks in multi-zone: after hydration the
study-guide router intercepts clicks and can't find /practice, /mocks etc.
Replace with plain <a> tags pointing to the main domain.

fix: resilient lockout — startup column creation + timezone-safe auth

Ensures lockout columns exist at app startup via raw SQL ALTER TABLE
(safety net for migration timing). Auth route now handles timezone-naive
timestamps and gracefully degrades if columns aren't available yet.

Replace text-input priority entry with star-based marking on existing tasks.

isMediumPriority field throughout data model, sync, and UI.

fix: Resolve net_server test deadlock and flaky accept_and_respond

- Remove duplicate TEST_MUTEX.lock() calls that caused deadlock (Mutex is non-reentrant)
- Handle PoisonError with unwrap_or_else to prevent cascading test failures
- Rewrite accept_and_respond: use OS-assigned ephemeral port, add read timeout,
  use channel-based join timeout to prevent indefinite hangs
- Approve new insta snapshots for net_requires_inject and linear_leak_is_rejected

commit.message:
fix: repair corrupted service files and test suite (#78)

* docs: rewrite README with composable infrastructure positioning

Lead with optionality — every feature is a layer you opt into, nothing
assumes anything else is running. Added "Use What You Need" table,
competitive positioning section, mood-aware queue highlight, integrations
section, and contributing gates overview.

Replaces feature bullet list with architecture-first framing that
differentiates from TUI-only players and throwaway MCP scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🎵 California Love — 2Pac
https://open.spotify.com/track/5bMRDvmHOaTeHsUbGnbcBt

* refactor: extract SpotifyAuthManager, SpotifyPlayerService, SpotifyDiscoveryService from SpotifyService for #71

🎵 Birthday Song — 2 Chainz
https://open.spotify.com/track/2grjqo0eY8IOTnRLBLgSbf

* fix: repair corrupted service files and test suite for #71

The refactor commit introduced HTML-encoded PHP files (<?php as <?php),
random Unicode corruption in variable names, wrong mock syntax (PHPUnit
style on Mockery mocks), and missing auth propagation to sub-services.

- Decode HTML entities in all 4 service files
- Fix ~15 corrupted identifiers (datapection, seedArtistuariIds, etc.)
- Switch test mocks to Mockery syntax (shouldReceive/andReturn)
- Inject mock auth into player and discovery sub-services
- Remove calls to private ensureValidToken() from external classes
- Add SpotifyAuthManagerTest with proper config-based token path setup

473 tests passing, 0 failures.

🎵 Now playing: Queen - Under Pressure
💿 Album: Unknown
🔗 Track: https://open.spotify.com/track/5AZGbqiIK5t1jrWSPT7k8X

* fix: repair last PHPStan corruption in DiscoveryService

Remove stray `&lower` in energy comparison (line 379).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(m7): Flag Service — CRUD, evaluation, CGo bridge, audit trail (#13)

* feat(m7): complete flag service — CRUD, evaluation, CGo bridge, audit trail

Phase 1 (milestones 1.28-1.30):
- Pure-Go MurmurHash3 x86 32-bit matching Rust implementation
- CGo bridge to Rust FFI: all 10,000 hash test vectors match
- ConnectRPC handlers: CreateFlag, GetFlag, UpdateFlag, ListFlags
- EvaluateFlag/EvaluateFlags with deterministic hash-based rollout
- Monotonic rollout: increasing percentage never evicts existing users
- PromoteToExperiment (mocked — awaiting Agent-5 CRUD API)
- PostgreSQL store with pgx pool + cursor-based pagination
- Build-tag swap: pure-Go (!cgo || !has_ffi) / CGo (cgo && has_ffi)

Phase 2 (audit trail + stale detection):
- flag_audit_trail table + stale_flags SQL view
- AuditStore interface with Postgres and mock implementations
- Audit recording on create/update/enable/disable/promote
- Stale flag detection: flags at 100% rollout unchanged >90 days
- Internal HTTP endpoints: /internal/flags/audit, /internal/flags/stale

SQL migrations: 002_feature_flags.sql, 003_flag_audit_trail.sql

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(m7): update status — milestones 1.28-1.30 complete

Phase 1 (CRUD + eval + CGo bridge) and Phase 2 (audit trail + stale detection)
are complete. 10K hash test vectors pass through both pure-Go and CGo bridge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(m7): update status with PR #13 reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(m4a): implement CUPED variance reduction with golden-file validation

- Implement cuped_adjust() in experimentation-stats with fail-fast assert_finite
- Formula: Y_adj = Y - θ(X - X̄) where θ = Cov(Y,X)/Var(X) on pooled sample
- Uses ddof=1 (matching R's var()/cov()) for all variance/covariance computations
- 5 golden-file datasets generated via numpy/scipy, validated to 1e-6 tolerance
- Fix snapshot.rs key separator: colon → null byte to prevent prefix collisions
- Add test_prefix_ids_do_not_collide regression test for snapshot keys

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: implement Plan 048 — test coverage meaningfulness (#142)

* feat: implement Plan 048 — test coverage meaningfulness

Improve test signal quality with 241 new tests, fix anti-patterns, and
add CI gates for regression and lint checks.

Phase 1 (P0):
- WI-1: Fix 1 tautological assertion, 17 over-broad exception catches,
  enhance ~15 smoke-only tests, add anti-pattern lint script
- WI-2: 25 unit tests for modal_app.py pure functions (0% → ~60%)
- WI-3: 41 tests for sweep_trigger, cloud_executor, supabase_client
- WI-4: 77 contract tests for supabase_store (14% → 80%) and
  supabase_registry (16% → 98%)
- WI-5: 16 native kernel parity tests (12 conditional + 4 mock-based)

Phase 2 (P1):
- WI-6: 12 determinism/flag-parity/golden-snapshot regression tests
- WI-7: 33 golden-output and comparison runner integrity tests
- WI-8: 28 benchmark utility contract tests

Phase 3 (P2):
- WI-9: Consolidate duplicated factories into tests/conftest.py and
  tests/banking/conftest.py, update 8 consuming test files
- WI-10: Add test-antipatterns + regression-tests CI jobs to
  quality-benchmarks.yml, native-parity job to extended-benchmarks.yml

Coverage: 85% → 87%, Tests: 5611 → 5852, Anti-pattern violations: 0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback on anti-pattern lint and native parity CI

P1: Anti-pattern lint now detects Exception inside tuples (e.g.
pytest.raises((SystemExit, Exception))). Fixed the one violation in
test_run_coverage.py by narrowing to (SystemExit, yaml.YAMLError).

P2: Native parity CI job now builds the Rust extension (dtolnay/rust-toolchain
+ maturin develop) and is blocking (removed continue-on-error: true).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: CI failures in native-parity and regression-tests jobs

- Native parity: use `maturin build` + `pip install` instead of
  `maturin develop` which requires a virtualenv
- Regression tests: guard test_cli_treynor.py with pytest.importorskip
  since the treynor CLI module is not committed to the repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
